rawCommits.subject: Wait for the virtualized tail before revealing chat
rawCommits.body:
Feed a bottom-window layout signature from ChatMessageList into useScrollAnchor so the panel no longer reveals as soon as outer scrollHeight looks stable. The initial settle path now waits until the virtualized bottom range reaches the tail and stops changing before chrome becomes visible.

This keeps the existing active auto-follow behavior, but removes the visible post-reveal scroll correction on first open. It also adds focused hook coverage for tail reachability and ongoing bottom-window measurement churn.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Apply rustfmt formatting across server handlers, billing, orgs, sessions, store, tasks, and link crates
rawCommits.body: Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Merge branch 'main' of https://github.com/cypher-asi/aura-os
Remove dead agent editor system prompt props
Stabilize eval shell expectations
Run desktop validate only for PRs
Bump CI Node version to 22
Align CI Node version with local
Preserve encoded updater public key
Stabilize workflow eval stats assertions
Set up CI keychain for iOS signing
Float desktop update banner above taskbar
Run iOS beta lane on main pushes
Add dedicated mobile workspace shell
Refine mobile agent and project workflows
Harden mobile and desktop regression coverage
Update mobile smoke eval contract
Polish mobile execution view
Fix desktop regression test coverage
Generate daily release changelogs
Update stale desktop eval workbench assertions
Refine changelog prompt toward editorial release notes
Remove Cursor reference from changelog prompt
Refactor machine recovery to always delete then reprovision
rawCommits.body:
The old recovery flow called the same idempotent POST /v1/agents
endpoint used for initial provisioning, which often resulted in the
swarm keeping the same machine mapping. This made recovery ineffective.

The new flow enforces an explicit two-step sequence with no fallbacks:
1. DELETE /v1/agents/:id - tear down the existing machine (404 = OK)
2. POST /v1/agents - provision a fresh machine
3. Poll until ready (inline, not background-spawned)

Each phase broadcasts real-time progress over WebSocket so the frontend
can show granular status updates (deleting, provisioning, waiting,
ready, error) instead of relying on timed polling follow-ups.

Backend:
- Add delete_swarm_agent() helper with 404-is-OK semantics
- Split old reprovision_remote_agent into provision_remote_agent (for
  create) and recover_remote_agent_pipeline (delete+provision+wait)
- Extract persist_vm_id() to reduce duplication
- Add wait_for_swarm_agent_ready_with_broadcast() for recovery path
- Remove vm_id_changed and message fields from response/struct
- broadcast_recovery_phase() emits typed WS events at each step

Frontend:
- AgentEnvironment subscribes to RemoteAgentStateChanged WS events
  with action=recover for real-time phase-driven recovery notices
- Remove polling follow-up logic (RECOVERY_FOLLOWUP_DELAY_MS, etc.)
- Remove vm_id_changed/message handling from API types and UI
- Extend RemoteAgentStateChanged event type with action/phase fields

Tests:
- Mock swarm DELETE endpoint in all recovery tests
- Replace same-mapping test with delete-failure and 404-success tests
- Update frontend tests for WS-driven recovery flow

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Hide chat viewport until first reveal completes
rawCommits.body:
Add a solid veil over the message viewport while the initial chat settle path is still running, so long histories cannot flash visible text even if virtualization or final scroll correction does one last internal pass before reveal.

This keeps the shell mounted for measurement, leaves the input and queue gating unchanged, and adds focused panel coverage for the veil lifecycle.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Clear recovery notice on success instead of showing it
rawCommits.body: Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Merge branch 'main' of https://github.com/cypher-asi/aura-os
Refactor first-open agent chat reveal flow
rawCommits.body:
Extract conversation snapshot and viewport phase ownership out of the scroll hook so the first visible frame is driven by explicit data, layout, and reveal state instead of the old settling heuristics. The chat list now reports semantic tail-layout readiness, while useScrollAnchor is reduced to active bottom pinning and follow behavior.

Also gate the standalone agent index redirect on agent-list readiness so the route does not briefly choose the wrong empty/redirect branch during first load, and add focused tests for the new viewport phase and simplified scroll contract.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Speed up shared desktop Cargo rebuilds
rawCommits.body:
Move aura-os desktop and release workflows onto a shared Cargo target directory so the desktop app and harness sidecar can reuse compiled artifacts. Also strip dev and test debug info to shorten default local feedback loops without changing release builds.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Merge branch 'main' of https://github.com/cypher-asi/aura-os
Align CI and local runtime parity on latest toolchains.
rawCommits.body:
Move workflow validation into shared repo scripts and pin the current Node, Rust, Java, and Ruby versions so local checks fail the same way as GitHub Actions.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Split shell dependencies into lazy interface chunks.
rawCommits.body:
Keep startup JS small by loading app surfaces, modal chrome, and desktop window UI only when the active flow needs them, while preserving the existing perf guardrails.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Optimize startup, auth, and loading performance.
rawCommits.body:
Reduce eager shell imports, remove duplicate auth validation work, defer noncritical realtime startup, and add measurable interface performance guardrails so the app stays fast as the codebase grows.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Show credits balance from the desktop taskbar.
rawCommits.body:
Add a right-side chevron beside the credits shortcut so users can reveal their current Z balance without opening the credits modal.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Display context utilization in chat input bar and support session reset.
rawCommits.body:
The AURA proxy router returns context_utilization on each assistant_message_end SSE event. This change extracts that value and displays it as a percentage in the ChatInputBar info bar, with color warnings at 70%% and 90%% thresholds. A new session button lets users clear the context by destroying the server-side chat session and wiping the local message list. The backend gains reset-session endpoints for both standalone agents and project agent instances, plus a new_session flag on SendChatRequest that skips loading stored conversation history so the next message starts with a clean context window.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Fix chat paste handling while conversations settle.
rawCommits.body:
Preserve mixed clipboard text in the composer and keep the input usable before the viewport reveal finishes.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Match right credits chevron expansion to the taskbar pattern.
rawCommits.body:
Place the revealed Z balance between the right chevron and credits icon so the chevron shifts outward the same way the left taskbar control does.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Wire context utilization and session reset into desktop agent windows.
rawCommits.body: Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Fix chat lane readiness and desktop shell startup.
rawCommits.body:
Keep the chat lane revealed after its first successful layout so scrolling history and sending follow-ups no longer collapse the panel, and preload the route-matched app while restoring the real desktop outlet instead of the web placeholder.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Fix duplicate project panel fallback rendering.
rawCommits.body:
Stop rendering route children through the lazy main-panel and provider fallbacks so Projects no longer appears once before the app shell resolves and then again inside the loaded shell.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Remove shell route fallbacks and dev double-mounting.
rawCommits.body:
Drop visible suspense fallbacks from the shell route path and stop wrapping the root in StrictMode so Projects no longer flashes placeholder states or mounts twice during startup.

rawCommits.subject: Drop redundant Ctx label from context utilization indicator.

rawCommits.subject: Keep desktop agent chat chrome stable during switches.
rawCommits.body:
Move the main-panel background and right-edge divider onto the persistent desktop host so agent changes only swap content, and add coverage that the host stays mounted across sidekick content swaps.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Keep agent chat pinned during history loads.
rawCommits.body:
Reset the viewport settle cycle for newly loaded histories, hide populated transcripts until the bottom anchor settles, and re-anchor idle sends to avoid scroll jank.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Default tasks sidekick to the tasks panel.
rawCommits.body:
Switch the shared sidekick to the tasks tab when entering the Tasks app so it no longer inherits the terminal section, and cover the app-store transition with a focused test.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Keep desktop launches pinned to the desktop workspace.
rawCommits.body:
Restore the desktop background by preventing the shell surface from painting over it.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Harden standalone agent SSE parsing.
rawCommits.body:
Handle real-world SSE framing and fall back to tagged payload event types so standalone agent replies keep streaming even when the transport uses generic event names or incomplete frame delimiters.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Restore last app startup routing.
rawCommits.body:
Stop forcing desktop-capable launches to /desktop so the app reopens where the user left off.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Fix chat messages hidden by unbounded viewport settling loop.
rawCommits.body:
The scroll-anchor settling loop could spin indefinitely without reaching the bottom, keeping chromeReady false and the message content area stuck at visibility:hidden. Cap settle attempts at 12 frames so the content always reveals, and replace visibility:hidden with a translucent opacity so messages stay visible even during settling.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Keep stream events visible until history catches up.
rawCommits.body:
The hydrateToStream:false path cleared stream events as soon as historyLastMessageAt changed, but the server may not have persisted the assistant reply yet. Guard the clear with a message-count check so live stream data stays visible until history actually covers it.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Surface remote state fetch errors in AgentEnvironment UI
rawCommits.body:
Previously, 404 and other fetch failures were silently swallowed, leaving the status card blank. Now the component shows a descriptive message and the profile-status-store marks failed agents as error.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Rebuild chat layout as bottom-anchored virtualized architecture
rawCommits.body:
Replace the render-then-fix-scroll approach with anchor-based scroll
reconciliation that eliminates all visible jank:

- Anchor-based scroll manager tracks message ID + viewport offset instead
  of naive scrollTop = scrollHeight, corrects via delta in useLayoutEffect
- Persistent height cache with type-based estimates feeds the virtualizer
  so initial layout is stable without a settling loop
- Normalized message store (Map by ID + ordered IDs per thread) replaces
  flat event arrays, with a separate view model for pagination/unread state
- Cursor-based pagination endpoint on the backend enables load older
  with anchor-preserving prepend on the frontend
- Floating new messages pill when user is scrolled up
- Media space reservation via aspect-ratio placeholders prevents late
  layout shifts from images
- StreamingBubble height handoff pre-seeds the cache when stream ends,
  preventing jumps on the streaming-to-persisted row transition

Deletes useChatViewportPhase (RAF settle loop + opacity hack) and the
sentinel-based useScrollAnchor in favor of the new architecture.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Fix chat bottom anchoring during initial history load
rawCommits.body:
Wire the virtualized chat list back into scroll-anchor reconciliation so
initial history hydration and later row remeasurement keep the viewport
pinned to the latest message without a visible re-scroll.

- propagate content-height changes from ChatMessageList into
  useScrollAnchorV2 and allow immediate reconciliation before paint
- add row ResizeObserver support so late height changes preserve the
  current anchor instead of leaving the viewport at a stale offset
- reconnect ChatPanel to the reconciliation callback that Phase 1 added
  but never actually used
- replace stale tests for the deleted viewport-phase and old scroll hook
  with coverage for the current bottom-anchor architecture

This fixes the main gap from the original plan: chats can now settle at
the bottom as history and measurements arrive instead of rendering in an
old position and correcting later.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Hide first-load chat history until bottom anchor is primed
rawCommits.body:
Prevent uncached chat history from appearing at the wrong scroll position
before the initial bottom-anchor pass finishes.

- add a first-load reveal gate in ChatPanel that keeps cold-load threads
  visually hidden while the initial bottom alignment is prepared
- reveal the thread only after ChatMessageList completes its first
  immediate content-height reconciliation
- keep the thread mounted during priming so measurement and virtualization
  still work correctly
- preserve the fast path for warm revisits where history is already ready
  on first render
- add tests covering cold-load hidden reveal and unchanged warm-load
  behavior

This fixes the remaining first-load jank where content would appear and
then scroll down into place.

Made-with: Cursor

Drop the remaining empty-chat and loading-copy placeholders so empty

This keeps the first-load reveal/fade behavior while removing the extra

rawCommits.subject: Stabilize streamed chat scroll behavior

Keep the chat viewport from fighting browser anchoring and duplicate height reconciliation while streamed output is growing. Reserve the streaming phase indicator so thinking-to-text transitions no longer cause the message list to jerk during live updates.

rawCommits.subject: Stream sidekick specs and tasks as they are created.
rawCommits.body:
Broadcast spec and task creation over the shared event socket so the sidekick updates before an LLM run finishes, while keeping the final refetch as a reconciliation fallback.

Mirror saved artifact events from runtime tool results onto the shared event bus so Specs and Tasks update in the sidekick even when create_spec or create_task runs through the runtime bridge instead of the REST handlers.

rawCommits.subject: Persist websocket artifact saves in the sidekick store.

rawCommits.subject: Show automation progress in the sidekick Run button.

rawCommits.subject: Improve chat height estimates for rich content

Start new chat rows closer to their final rendered size so tool-heavy, markdown-heavy, thinking, artifact, and image messages do not appear low and then jump upward after measurement. Add focused coverage for complex assistant output and image-heavy user messages.

rawCommits.body:
Persist the full non-desktop path so Aura can reopen the user’s previous deep-link target instead of only falling back to the last app, and document the new feedback requirements reference.

rawCommits.subject: Smooth chat streaming handoff transitions
rawCommits.body:
Keep live streaming output pinned in place by reconciling streaming bubble height immediately and carrying that measured height into the saved assistant row. Remove the extra post-stream bottom scroll so the end-of-turn handoff does not snap down after the final message lands.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Load the desktop shell on the saved route.
rawCommits.body:
Persist the last valid shell route into native desktop state so startup and dev-server handoff can open the correct path immediately instead of flashing the default app first.

rawCommits.subject: Deduplicate assistant stream handoff messages.
rawCommits.body:
Replace the synthetic assistant boundary placeholder with the persisted message_end event so new-agent chats do not briefly show the same reply twice.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Update project startup flow and transient chat rendering.
rawCommits.body:
Ensure new projects and local agent instances provision their workspace directories, avoid empty-state flashes while project data is still hydrating, and deduplicate optimistic chat rows that briefly render twice before persisted history catches up.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Stabilize sidekick chat lane resizing.
rawCommits.body:
Treat sidekick width changes as a resize session so the chat preserves one scroll invariant and remeasures once after the drag settles instead of fighting layout on every wrapped row.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Stream spec and task creation updates in real time.
rawCommits.body:
Process-linked runs now emit saved-artifact events as each spec/task is persisted, and the UI merges those events plus optimistic task placeholders so new work appears immediately instead of after the full run completes.

rawCommits.subject: Smooth live text streaming.
rawCommits.body:
Buffer streamed text on a short cadence so the UI updates in steadier batches, reducing janky jumps while still flushing quickly at natural boundaries.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Refine streamed text to reveal one word at a time.
rawCommits.body:
Pace assistant output through a hybrid word-by-word scheduler so streaming feels smoother, catches up under backlog, and still flushes full content at terminal boundaries.

rawCommits.subject: Stabilize streamed output handoff.
rawCommits.body:
Keep placeholder assistant messages on the streaming-safe render path and avoid rendering an empty live bubble after text has already settled, so streamed content no longer jumps or briefly misformats at completion.

rendered.title: Mobile Workspace Shell and Chat Performance Overhaul
rendered.intro: Today's release introduces a dedicated mobile workspace shell with streamlined navigation and rebuilds the chat system with bottom-anchored virtualization to eliminate scroll jank. We've also enhanced release infrastructure with automated changelog generation and improved CI reliability.

items.text: Added dedicated mobile workspace shell with organization switching, project navigation drawer, and streamlined agent workflows optimized for on-the-go work
Refined mobile agent and project workflows with improved agent selection, project setup flows, and execution view layouts for mobile devices
Enhanced mobile regression test coverage with visual validation and responsive layout checks
Rebuilt chat layout with bottom-anchored virtualized architecture that eliminates visible scroll jank during history loads and streaming
Implemented smooth streaming text reveal with word-by-word pacing and improved height estimation for rich content messages
Added context utilization display in chat input bar with session reset capability and real-time streaming of spec and task creation updates
Stabilized chat viewport during sidekick resizing and improved streaming handoff transitions to prevent layout jumps
Optimized startup performance with lazy shell imports, reduced auth validation overhead, and added bundle size budgets with performance guardrails
Improved desktop route persistence and shell loading to restore the last valid route on startup instead of defaulting to the home app
Enhanced agent recovery flow with explicit delete-then-provision sequence and real-time WebSocket progress updates
Added automated daily changelog generation with AI-powered release notes for both nightly and stable channels
Upgraded CI toolchain to Node 22, Java 21, and improved Cargo build caching with shared target directories for faster desktop rebuilds
Aligned CI and local runtime parity with pinned toolchain versions and improved iOS beta deployment automation

highlights: Mobile workspace shell with streamlined navigation and agent workflows
Bottom-anchored chat virtualization eliminates scroll jank
Automated AI-powered changelog generation for releases
Significant startup performance improvements with lazy loading
